_meta.note: Edit editorial block per issue. Everything else (drivers, sectors, gaps, opportunities, risks) is generated from system_model.json + rbi_sibc.ts. Do not manually duplicate content from those files here.

tldr: ₹204.8L Cr total bank credit — +₹25.8L Cr added in 12 months, the largest absolute addition in this dataset. The credit cycle is genuinely accelerating.

Gold loans +128.8% YoY: this is demand migration, not a boom. RBI's November 2023 risk-weight increase repriced unsecured credit — borrowers moved to gold, which is secured, RBI-friendly, and still competitive.
Micro & Small enterprises +31.2% — three times last year's rate. The growth is real, but part of it is GST/UDYAM formalisation making previously-invisible businesses bankable for the first time.
Engineering credit +35.9% and accelerating for three consecutive periods. PLI commitments are translating into actual factory borrowing. This has runway.
editorial.system_narrative: Three things moved the credit system this month — and they are connected. RBI's November 2023 risk-weight increase on unsecured lending repriced credit cards out of reach for many borrowers. That demand didn't disappear. It migrated to gold loans, which are secured, RBI-friendly, and now growing at 128.8% YoY. Separately, five years of GST registration and UDYAM enrolment are showing up in credit data for the first time — businesses that always existed but were invisible to formal lending. That is Micro & Small credit growing at 31.2%, not the economy suddenly accelerating. And PLI commitments are translating into factory borrowing — Engineering credit up 60% in two years. Three different forces, three different credit markets, all moving simultaneously. This is not a rising tide lifting all boats. It is three specific tides, each with its own logic.

bullets: Gold loan growth rate — does +128.8% sustain or begin decelerating as the market matures and early movers saturate the accessible TAM?
Micro & Small base effect — March 2026 data will show whether +31.2% holds or was partially a January seasonal bump on top of the formalisation tailwind.
Shipping series — watch for RBI restatement or explanation of the January 2026 discontinuity. The ₹7.2L Cr cannot have simply vanished.
PSL Housing — +37.9% acceleration needs confirmation. One data point does not make a trend; March 2026 will tell us if PMAY demand is structural.
